Sydneya lancastrae, commonly known as the Lancashire heath, is a type of heath plant that belongs to the family Ericaceae.

The Lancashire heath has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease.

The bioactive constituents of the Lancashire heath include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The Lancashire heath is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
